one_time_description: Subsequent event: settlement regarding litigation with former members of council entitling the Band to $3,475,431 compensation for damages (recoverability unknown)
economic_notes: Band holds 25% in Pelltiq't Energy Group and 51% interest in Whispering Pines/A&T Limited Liability Partnership and Whispering Pines/Marwest Limited Liability Partnership (unrecorded due to no active operations). Investment in All Nations Trust Company (90,000 Class A common shares) and BC First Nations Gaming Partnership. Economic development segment generated $1,287,058 surplus in 2022 vs ($63,002) deficit in 2021, largely from partnership income of $1,573,377.
notes: Audit is qualified due to unrecorded investments in Pelltiq't Energy Group (25%), Whispering Pines/A&T LLP (51%), and Whispering Pines/Marwest LLP (51%) — no audited financials available for these entities. Consolidated entity includes only Whispering Pines Clinton Indian Band Social Housing as a subsidiary. Subsequent event: $3,475,431 litigation settlement receivable with uncertain recoverability. No budget presented as Band does not prepare one. Deferred revenue increased significantly from $150,625 to $1,776,738. Partnership income of $1,573,377 is a major driver of 2022 surplus vs $121,923 in prior year. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($1,115,798) + CMHC ($11,887) + FNHA ($300,667) + Province of BC ($167,366) + FNESC ($46,175) = $1,641,893. expenses_housing includes band-owned housing ($57,783) + social housing ($17,373) = $75,156.
